prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo TIM-barrel scaffold (200–250 residues) that disrupts the ATPase activity of NS3 helicase from Hepatitis C virus genotype 1a by occupying the ATP-binding cleft without serving as an NS3 substrate. The designed protein should achieve Ki < 1 µM in a biochemical helicase assay. outline a methodology for: (1) RFdiffusion backbone generation with ATPase cleft geometry constraints from PDB 1A1V, (2) ProteinMPNN sequence design with electrostatic mimicry of ATP, (3) Rosetta docking and free energy perturbation scoring. Discuss selectivity versus human DEAD-box helicases.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: NS3 helicase/protease; organism: Hepatitis C virus genotype 1a; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: multifunctional replication complex protein (NTPase/helicase + serine protease)
